title: Set up Vitest for Next.js / React projects
summary: Complete Vitest setup — installs dependencies, configures vitest.config.ts with React plugin and jsdom, sets up Testing Library, coverage, path aliases, and package.json scripts.
declaration: Vitest is a Vite-native test runner that is fast, ESM-first, and has Jest-compatible APIs. I will set up Vitest for this project with React Testing Library. If you prefer Jest or another test framework, say so before I begin.
instructions: ## Set Up Vitest for Next.js / React ### 1. Pre-flight checks - Verify `package.json` exists. - Check if any test framework is already installed: look for `jest`, `vitest`, `mocha`, or `@testing-library/*` in dependencies/devDependencies. - If Jest is already configured, ask the user: migrate to Vitest or keep Jest? Do NOT mix both in the same project. - Determine the project type: - **Next.js**: Check for `next.config.js`/`next.config.mjs`. - **Vite**: Check for `vite.config.ts`. - **CRA or other**: Check build tooling. - Read `tsconfig.json` to identify path aliases (e.g., `@/*` → `./src/*`). These MUST be mirrored in vitest config. ### 2. Install dependencies - Core: ``` npm i -D vitest @vitejs/plugin-react ``` - Test environment (choose one): - **jsdom** (recommended for most projects — wider compatibility): ``` npm i -D jsdom ``` - **happy-dom** (faster but less complete DOM implementation — use for speed-critical large test suites): ``` npm i -D happy-dom ``` - Default recommendation: use **jsdom** unless the user specifically requests happy-dom. - Testing Library (for React component tests): ``` npm i -D @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom @testing-library/user-event ``` - Coverage: ``` npm i -D @vitest/coverage-v8 ``` ### 3. Create vitest.config.ts - Create `vitest.config.ts` at the project root. Do NOT put test config inside `vite.config.ts` for Next.js projects — keep them separate to avoid conflicts with Next.js build. - For Vite-based projects, you CAN extend the existing `vite.config.ts` using `mergeConfig` if preferred. #### For Next.js projects: ```typescript import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config' import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react' import path from 'path' export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()], test: { environment: 'jsdom', globals: true, setupFiles: ['./vitest.setup.ts'], include: ['**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}'], exclude: ['node_modules', '.next', 'e2e'], css: true, }, resolve: { alias: { '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'), }, }, }) ``` - CRITICAL: Read `tsconfig.json` `compilerOptions.paths` and replicate EVERY alias in `resolve.alias`. Mismatched aliases cause `Cannot find module '@/...'` errors. - If the project uses `src/` directory, alias `@` → `./src`. If no `src/`, alias `@` → `.`. - Adjust the alias map to match the actual tsconfig paths. #### For Vite projects: ```typescript /// import { defineConfig } from 'vite' import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react' import path from 'path' export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()], test: { environment: 'jsdom', globals: true, setupFiles: ['./vitest.setup.ts'], include: ['**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx,js,jsx}'], css: true, }, resolve: { alias: { '@': path.resolve(__dirname, './src'), }, }, }) ``` ### 4. Create vitest.setup.ts - Create `vitest.setup.ts` at the project root: ```typescript import '@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest' ``` - This registers custom matchers like `toBeInTheDocument()`, `toHaveTextContent()`, etc. - If the project needs additional setup (e.g., mocking `next/router`, `next/navigation`), add mocks here: ```typescript import '@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest' // Mock next/navigation for App Router projects vi.mock('next/navigation', () => ({ useRouter: () => ({ push: vi.fn(), replace: vi.fn(), back: vi.fn(), prefetch: vi.fn(), }), useSearchParams: () => new URLSearchParams(), usePathname: () => '/', })) ``` ### 5. Add TypeScript types - If `globals: true` is set, add Vitest types to `tsconfig.json`: ```json { "compilerOptions": { "types": ["vitest/globals"] } } ``` - This enables `describe`, `it`, `expect`, `vi` without explicit imports. - Do NOT remove existing types — append `"vitest/globals"` to the array. ### 6. Add package.json scripts - Add these scripts to `package.json` (do NOT overwrite existing scripts): ```json { "scripts": { "test": "vitest run", "test:watch": "vitest", "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage", "test:ui": "vitest --ui" } } ``` - `vitest run` = single run (CI-friendly). `vitest` = watch mode (development). - For Vitest UI, also install: `npm i -D @vitest/ui`. ### 7. Create an example test - Create a sample test to verify the setup works. Place it next to an existing component or in a `__tests__` directory. - Example for a React component: ```typescript import { render, screen } from '@testing-library/react' import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest' import HomePage from '@/app/page' // adjust path describe('HomePage', () => { it('renders without crashing', () => { render() expect(document.body).toBeTruthy() }) }) ``` - If the component has complex dependencies (providers, context, fetch), create a simpler test first to validate the setup. ### 8. Run and verify - Run: `npx vitest run`. - If tests pass, the setup is complete. - If tests fail, check the error output carefully and apply fixes from the common errors section below. - Run coverage to verify: `npx vitest run --coverage`. ### 9. Testing API routes and Server Actions (Next.js) - API routes and Server Actions run in Node.js, not the browser. Test them with `environment: 'node'`. - Use per-file environment override with a comment at the top of the test file: ```typescript // @vitest-environment node import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest' ``` - For API routes, import the handler and test it directly: ```typescript // @vitest-environment node import { GET, POST } from '@/app/api/example/route' import { NextRequest } from 'next/server' describe('GET /api/example', () => { it('returns 200', async () => { const req = new NextRequest('http://localhost:3000/api/example') const res = await GET(req) expect(res.status).toBe(200) }) }) ``` - For Server Actions, import and call them directly. Mock any database or external calls. ### 10. Coverage configuration - To customize coverage, add to `vitest.config.ts`: ```typescript test: { coverage: { provider: 'v8', reporter: ['text', 'html', 'lcov'], exclude: [ 'node_modules/', '.next/', '**/*.config.*', '**/*.d.ts', 'vitest.setup.ts', ], thresholds: { statements: 80, branches: 80, functions: 80, lines: 80, }, }, } ``` - Add `coverage/` to `.gitignore`. ### Common errors & fixes - **'Cannot use import statement outside a module'** → Missing `@vitejs/plugin-react` plugin in vitest config. Ensure `plugins: [react()]` is present. - **'Cannot find module @/...'** → Path alias mismatch. Compare `tsconfig.json` `paths` with `resolve.alias` in vitest config. They MUST match exactly. - **'document is not defined'** or **'window is not defined'** → Missing `environment: 'jsdom'` (or `'happy-dom'`). The default environment is `node` which has no DOM. - **'toBeInTheDocument is not a function'** → Missing setup file. Ensure `setupFiles: ['./vitest.setup.ts']` is set and the file imports `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest`. - **'ReferenceError: describe is not defined'** → Either set `globals: true` in vitest config, or explicitly import `{ describe, it, expect }` from `'vitest'`. - **'TypeError: vi.fn is not a function'** → Same as above — `vi` needs `globals: true` or explicit import. - **CSS/SCSS import errors** → Add `css: true` to test config, or mock CSS files: ```typescript // vitest.config.ts test: { css: true, // or css: { modules: { classNameStrategy: 'non-scoped' } } } ``` - **Next.js Image component errors** → Mock `next/image` in setup file: ```typescript vi.mock('next/image', () => ({ default: (props: any) => , })) ``` - **Slow tests** → Switch from `jsdom` to `happy-dom` for faster execution. Or use `--pool=forks` for CPU-heavy tests. - **Coverage shows 0%** → Ensure `@vitest/coverage-v8` is installed. Check that test files are not in the `exclude` list. ### What NOT to do - Do NOT use Jest config syntax (`jest.config.js`, `moduleNameMapper`, `transform`) in vitest config — Vitest has its own API. - Do NOT skip setting `environment: 'jsdom'` — the default is `node`, which breaks ALL DOM/React tests silently. - Do NOT mix Jest and Vitest in the same project — they conflict on globals, matchers, and mock APIs. - Do NOT put vitest config inside `next.config.js` — it does not belong there. - Do NOT use `@testing-library/jest-dom` without the `/vitest` import path — use `@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest` for proper Vitest matcher integration. - Do NOT forget to add `vitest/globals` to `tsconfig.json` types when using `globals: true` — TypeScript will show errors for `describe`, `it`, etc. - Do NOT create test files with `.test.js` extension in a TypeScript project — use `.test.ts` or `.test.tsx` for consistency. - Do NOT ignore path alias setup — this is the #1 cause of 'module not found' errors in Vitest with Next.js.
